project_overview.project_name: Oromia Forested Landscape Program – Emission Reduction Project
project_overview.project_development_objective: The project will generate measured, reported and verified Emissions Reductions (ER) from reduced deforestation, forest degradation, enhancement of forest carbon stocks (REDD+), agriculture and other land use sectors that meet the GHG accounting requirements of the BioCF ISFL in the Oromia State and will distribute ER payments in accordance with an agreed benefit sharing plan.
project_overview.total_financing: US$61.95 million (US$60 million ERPA + US$1.95 million grants)
project_overview.beneficiaries: 400,000 people living in communities adjacent to forests, 2,000 communities, 25,000 people in registered cooperatives, 10,000 smallholder farmers

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - ongoing instability in western Wollega cluster zone, ethnic conflicts, OLF-Shane operations, territorial disputes with Somali region

key_components.component_name: Emission Reductions payments distributed in accordance with agreed Benefit Sharing Plan
key_components.description: Purchase of emission reduction credits and distribution of net revenues according to benefit sharing plan

key_components.component_name: Comprehensive Measurement, reporting and verification (MRV) system and program management including Safeguards Management system
key_components.description: Program management, E&S standards implementation, and improvement of MRV system for livestock sector

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ethiopia Forest Development (EFD) with Oromia Environmental Protection Authority (OEPA) and Oromia REDD+ Coordination Unit (ORCU)
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Oromia Regional State Vice-President chairs OFLP Steering Committee, multisector coordination through Rural Development Cluster
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF, national procedures for certain activities
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Biannual monitoring reports, third-party verification, MRV system for forest and livestock sectors

identified_risks.risk_description: Unstable political situation, social unrest, conflict in Tigray spreading to other regions including Oromia, ethnic tensions and violence
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Framework approach allowing flexibility, remote monitoring, strategic communication, enhanced transparency, security management plan

identified_risks.risk_description: OLF-Shane operations in West/Kellem Wollega, ethnic violence, territorial disputes, civilian casualties and displacement
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Security risk assessment conducted, proportionate security management plan developed and disclosed

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited institutional capacity, multisectoral coordination challenges, staff turnover, weak enforcement
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Capacity building, grants for operational costs, coordination platforms, training programs

identified_risks.risk_description: New benefit sharing system, delays in MRV activities, absence of legal framework for ER ownership, weak procurement capacity
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: BSP operational manual, legal framework development, procurement training, close supervision

identified_risks.risk_description: Jurisdictional scale implementation, fragile environment, complex social relationships, potential for elite capture, GBV risks
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Updated ESMF, SESA, RPF, SEP, LMP, ESCP, GBV Action Plan, grievance redress mechanism, third-party monitoring

triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks
ESS4: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention
ESS6: Land Acquisition and Involuntary Resettlement
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - project-level GRM established, strengthened during implementation, separate GBV GRM guidelines
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Gender action plan developed, 60% of cooperative members targeted to be women, specific attention to women-headed households, gender mainstreaming guidelines

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project acknowledges nexus of FCV, natural resource use, and climate change. Addresses root causes affecting livelihoods and contributing to conflicts. Framework approach allows flexibility based on security situation.
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Ethiopia experiencing historic transition with new uncertainties, conflicts, and insecurities. Long-standing grievances exacerbated by climate change, resource competition, ethnic tensions, and governance issues.
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Security management plan developed and disclosed. Regular security risk assessments. Remote monitoring capabilities. Strategic communication and outreach. Enhanced transparency and citizen engagement.
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - significant internal displacement due to conflicts, ethnic violence, and forced displacement of people. Project areas affected by population movements and territorial disputes.
